topic: Overview evaluate-preface (5-question methodology block) — proposal package review
our_argument_summary: EXP-257 inserts a 5-question methodology preface on the Overview tab between the EXP-255 thesis-hero and the accuracy-math-section. Each question is a deep-link to the verdict bucket (or 6.6b dedup-analysis) that demonstrates the failure mode it detects. Reuses the eg-evaluate 5 questions verbatim (no invented content). Final of the EXP-255/256/257 Overview-rework trio for HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001 Item 4.

proposal_review.review_focus_per_proposal: Verbatim-question check, link-target demonstrative-value check, placement (climax preservation), redundancy with EXP-256, question-4 routing call, cross-tab UX, responsive layout, design-rubric application, anchor stability after EXP-253/254/255/256 land.

five_question_verbatim_audit.question_2_match: verbatim text; typography differs (analyst uses ’ entity, eg-evaluate uses straight ASCII apostrophe). Disclosed in analyst load_bearing_content_audit.
five_question_verbatim_audit.finding: All 5 questions are present in eg-evaluate as ... opening clauses. No invented content. Curly-vs-straight quote in Q2 is cosmetic and disclosed.

link_target_audit.targets_demonstrative_value: Reasonable. Q1 -> #stdmodel (non-discrimination is the canonical Std-Model-Explains failure mode). Q2 -> #selfcon (derivability failures live in Self-Contradicted). Q3 -> #refuted. Q4 -> #misleading (the bucket includes 4 explicit Unfalsifiable verdicts plus the Misleading group; analyst rejected #eg-falsifiability because that section is about OUR review's falsifiability conditions, not about evaluating the dome's claims). Q5 -> #dedup-analysis (6.6b is the structural dedup treatment; better than narrower targets like #2-2-tidal). The Q4 routing is a defensible judgment call but a defender could argue eg-falsifiability is the more on-the-nose methodology destination.

segment_EP1_html_anchor.verdict: OK — unique and present at line 1463 (post-EXP-255/256 layout).

segment_EP2_css_anchor.verdict: BROKEN — the analyst-claimed anchor literal does not exist. ISS-1571/1572/1573 (curmudgeon c1 review of EXP-255) closed by migrating verdict-bars CSS to ds-verdict-bars. EXP-257 was drafted at 2026-04-26T04:08Z, and the ds- prefix migration landed AFTER (the trio was queued at 2026-04-26T05:24Z). The proposal's anchor_uniqueness check was true at draft time but is invalidated by the prefix migration. A naive sed/string-replace integration will silently no-op for segment EP2.

design_system_naming_audit.closed_precedent_enforcing_prefix: ISS-1573 (severity moderate, status fixed) — 'EXP-255 new CSS classes missing ds- prefix (design system naming invariant broken).' Curmudgeon c1 H3 of EXP-255 explicitly required ds- prefix on all new component classes. EXP-257 violates the same invariant.
design_system_naming_audit.note: EXP-256 also shipped without ds- prefix (per ISS-1580 referencing fm-status-num directly), which is itself a follow-up gap; EXP-257 should not compound it.

wcag_contrast_audit.light_mode_ratio: 5.36:1 — passes WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text
wcag_contrast_audit.dark_mode_ratio: 4.23:1 — FAILS WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text
wcag_contrast_audit.none_qualify_as_large_text: 0.72-0.82rem ~ 11.5-13.1px; AA large-text threshold is 14pt-bold (~18.7px) or 18pt regular. None qualify, all need 4.5:1.
wcag_contrast_audit.closed_precedent: ISS-1580 (open, moderate) — EXP-256 falsifiability-module fm-status-num/fm-status-sub flagged for the SAME ink-3-on-card-bg dark-mode contrast gap. EXP-257 reproduces the defect.
fix_options: Switch the three uses to var(--ink-2) (computes 7.48:1 in dark mode, still distinct from --text and --heading) — simplest mechanical fix
Globally bump --ink-3 in dark mode (would also resolve ISS-1580; coordination required)

holistic_audit.visual_climax_preservation: Thesis-hero remains visual climax (display-scale serif + figure row). Preface uses body weight + neutral --ink-3 left rail, deliberately quieter than --accent left rail of falsifiability-module. Differentiation is real but reader vertical scroll burden goes up materially. Worth a post-integration fold check on 1366x768.
holistic_audit.redundancy_with_eg_evaluate: Hub-and-spoke pattern. Overview preface = orientation; tab eg-evaluate = long-form with medicine analogy. Caption 'Full preface ->' links the two. Acceptable.
holistic_audit.cross_tab_link_robustness: Q5 jumps overview->predictions. Site-wide pattern; back-button history machinery already handles it (lines 1810-1879 generate-html.js). No new risk.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Pure static HTML/CSS injection at build time. No template variables, no data dependencies.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Static — no dynamic substitutions. Robust against count drift in counts.total / failures fields.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Patch claim that anchor `.verdict-bars .vb-caption{...}` is unique with grep -c == 1 is FALSE in current main. The actual rule has been migrated to `.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{...}`.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: EXP-257 does not modify monitor.py or any data-fetching code. It is a presentation-layer addition only.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Not applicable — this is a presentation/UX patch, not a WIN claim.

holes_found.description: Segment EP2 CSS anchor `.verdict-bars .vb-caption{font-size:.9rem;color:var(--text);opacity:.75;margin:0 0 .8rem}` returns 0 grep hits in current build-scripts/generate-html.js. The ds- prefix migration (ISS-1571/1572/1573) renamed this to `.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{...}` after EXP-257 was drafted. A literal-anchor apply will silently no-op — same failure-mode class as ISS-1571 against EXP-255.
holes_found.recommendation: Re-anchor segment EP2 to `.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{font-size:.9rem;color:var(--text);opacity:.75;margin:0 0 .8rem}`. Decider should re-grep at integration time and refuse to apply if grep_count != 1.

holes_found.description: All 9 new CSS classes (.evaluate-preface, .ep-heading, .ep-caption, .ep-jump, .ep-rows, .ep-row, .ep-num, .ep-q, .ep-where) lack the ds- design-system prefix. The HTML insert references the same unprefixed class names. Violates the invariant that ISS-1573 (severity moderate, status fixed) explicitly enforced against EXP-255. Compounds the same prefix-gap that ISS-1580 indirectly evidences for EXP-256.
holes_found.recommendation: Rename to .ds-evaluate-preface, .ds-ep-heading, .ds-ep-caption, .ds-ep-jump, .ds-ep-rows, .ds-ep-row, .ds-ep-num, .ds-ep-q, .ds-ep-where in BOTH segment EP1 (HTML class= attributes) and segment EP2 (CSS selectors). Mechanical sed transform on the proposal text.

holes_found.description: Dark-mode WCAG AA contrast gap. .ep-heading (0.82rem weight:600), .ep-num (0.78rem weight:700), .ep-where (0.72rem weight:600 uppercase) all use color:var(--ink-3). In dark mode, --ink-3 (#8a8680) on --card-bg (#222240) computes to 4.23:1 — below the AA 4.5:1 threshold for normal text. None of the three font sizes qualify as WCAG large text (would need >=14pt bold ~ 18.7px). Reproduces ISS-1580 (still open) verbatim.
holes_found.recommendation: Switch the three .ep-* color declarations from var(--ink-3) to var(--ink-2) (computes 7.48:1 in dark mode, still typographically distinct from --text and --heading). Light mode is unaffected (5.36:1 -> 7.69:1). Alternatively, fix --ink-3 globally in dark mode and resolve ISS-1580 in the same pass.

holes_found.description: Question 2 typography divergence: patch uses model’s (curly entity), eg-evaluate uses model's (ASCII straight). Disclosed in analyst load_bearing_content_audit but creates micro-inconsistency between Overview-preface and Evaluation-Guide-tab body copy.
holes_found.recommendation: Either ASCII-apostrophe both surfaces or curly both — pick one. Lowest effort: ASCII in the patch, matching the eg-evaluate body copy.

holes_found.description: Caption link 'Full preface ->' lands on a closed `<details id="eg-evaluate">` element. Reader sees the section anchor but the long-form is collapsed by default. Site-wide pattern, but worth noting because the caption verbiage implies the long-form is one click away — it is two clicks (jump + expand).
holes_found.recommendation: Either rewrite the caption to set the expectation ('Read the long form ->') or augment showTab('evaluate') with an explicit details.open expansion for #eg-evaluate when arriving via the preface jump. Optional.

stronger_arguments.description: Add a tiny grep self-test to the proposal package: `grep -c <CSS_anchor>` and `grep -c <HTML_anchor>` BOTH return 1 at integration time, otherwise abort. This would have surfaced the prefix-migration gap automatically. ISS-1572 closed the post-condition tautology bug for FC1 — the same pattern (assert pre-state) belongs in any patch_segments-style proposal.

If the trio (EXP-255/256/257) ships together, sequence the integration: EXP-256 -> EXP-255 -> EXP-257 (or any order, but commit each separately and re-run anchor greps between). Concurrent application of multi-anchor patches against a moving target is exactly how this prefix-mismatch slipped past anchor_uniqueness.

kernel_of_truth.description: The 5-question methodology in eg-evaluate (with the medicine analogy) is genuinely the most generalizable scientific-thinking content in the review. It works whether the reader cares about the dome model or any other claim. Today, that content lives entirely in the Evaluation Guide tab — a casual Overview-only reader never sees the framework that the rest of the review applies. Promoting it to an Overview preface materially improves discoverability and gives the reader navigational shortcuts to the verdict buckets that demonstrate each failure mode.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The patch correctly delivers the rhetorical goal. The blockers are mechanical (broken CSS anchor, missing ds- prefix, dark-mode contrast). All three are fixable in one decider pass without touching content. Verdict change not warranted.

citations_verified: ISS-1571 (closed, critical) — EXP-255 FC1+FC2 broken find anchors / missing ds- prefix
ISS-1572 (closed, critical) — EXP-255 FC1 post-condition tautological
ISS-1573 (closed, moderate) — EXP-255 new CSS classes missing ds- prefix (precedent for the moderate finding here)
ISS-1580 (open, moderate) — EXP-256 falsifiability-module dark-mode WCAG contrast (precedent for the moderate finding here)
eg-evaluate verbatim 5 questions in docs/index.html line 1574
citations_unchecked: DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 design rubric (referenced by analyst, not pulled into this review for line-by-line application)

deeper_analysis: EXP-257 is the third member of the EXP-255/256/257 trio for the Overview rework HNOTE. The preface concept is sound and the question selection / link-target mapping is well-reasoned. The proposal's analytical thoroughness is high — 8 anticipated_objections with substantive responses, clear out_of_scope_explicit list, explicit hub-and-spoke rationale for redundancy with eg-evaluate. What the proposal misses is two-tier: (1) micro — the CSS anchor literal was correct at draft time but the ds- prefix migration (ISS-1571 family) landed in between draft and queue-push, invalidating the anchor; (2) meta — the proposal package format does not include any pre-write assertion (analogous to PROP-009 queue_id assertion for curmudgeon reviews). EXP-255 was bitten by exactly this pattern (FC1+FC2 anchors broken; FC1 post-condition tautological; ISS-1571/1572 closed). EXP-256 was bitten by the design-system prefix invariant (no closed issue but observable in current generate-html.js where the falsifiability-module is integrated without ds- prefix). EXP-257 reproduces both gaps. The right intervention is mechanical: re-anchor EP2, ds-prefix all new classes, switch ink-3->ink-2 in three CSS rules. None of these touch the rhetorical content. Curmudgeon recommends decider apply with these three fixes layered in; do NOT apply the patch verbatim, and do NOT block on the substance.
summary_for_decider: EXP-257 substance is approved (questions verbatim, link targets resolve, placement story sound). DO NOT apply patch verbatim — three mechanical fixes required: (1) CSS anchor must be `.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{...}` not `.verdict-bars .vb-caption{...}` (segment EP2 will silently no-op as written); (2) all 9 new CSS classes need ds- prefix to honor ISS-1573 invariant (rename in both segments); (3) switch ink-3->ink-2 on .ep-heading/.ep-num/.ep-where to clear dark-mode WCAG AA gap that ISS-1580 already flagged for EXP-256. Verdict change not recommended. Holistic concern noted: post-integration the Overview will stack 5 structured blocks; do a 1366x768 fold check.
defender_steelman: A defender of the patch as-written could argue (a) the ds- prefix migration is recent and the analyst could not have known; (b) EXP-256 shipped without prefix and only got follow-ups, so EXP-257 should be allowed the same treatment; (c) the CSS no-op is silent but harmless because the new CSS rules will still parse and apply (they're inserted at top level, not as a continuation), so visually the patch will still work — only the location of the new CSS rules in the file would be wrong, not their effect. Counter to (c): the new CSS will not be inserted at all if the anchor doesn't match — depending on the decider's apply mechanism, the patch either no-ops entirely or fails loudly. Either way the integration is wrong. Counter to (a)+(b): being recent doesn't excuse a curmudgeon-flaggable gap; ISS-1573 is closed precedent, not an open question.
